description: Letter in Hebrew from the community of Kiev on behalf of Yaʿaqov ben R. Hanukkah. He had been seized by Christian creditors (goyim) of his brother, who had been killed by brigands. Yaʿaqov had stood surety for the loan. The community had redeemed him by paying part of the debt. Now, apparently, they had sent Yaʿaqov to collect as much of what remained of the debt as possible from Jewish communities. He had evidently ended up in Fustat. Dated to the 10th century (Information from Mediterranean Society, IV, p. 348 and from Golb and Pritsak)
description_he: מכתב בעברית מקהילת קייב בשם יעקב בן ר' חנוכה. נלקח כערב לחובות אחיו שנהרג בידי שודדים. הקהילה פדתה אותו בתשלום חלקי. כעת נשלח לגבות שאר החוב מקהילות יהודיות. מתוארך למאה ה-10.
